+project: remove-prompts
+title: Remove the `sah prompt` CLI subcommand
+---
+## What
+Remove the entire `sah prompt` CLI subcommand (list / test / show / new / edit) and its wiring. This is a leaf removal — nothing else in the codebase depends on these command modules.
+
+Files to delete:
+- `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/prompt/` (whole dir: `mod.rs`, `cli.rs`, `list.rs`, `show.rs`, `test.rs`, `new.rs`, `edit.rs`, `display.rs`, `description.md`, `list_help.md`, `test_help.md`) — ~2867 LOC
+
+Files to edit:
+- `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/main.rs` — remove `handle_prompt_command` (around line 1279) and its dispatch arm `Some(("prompt", sub_matches)) => ...` (around line 548).
+- `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/cli.rs` — remove the `prompt` subcommand definition and update the top-level about/help text (lines ~70-213) that describe sah as "An MCP server for managing prompts as markdown files" and the `prompt list`/`prompt test` examples. Reframe help around skills/workflows.
+- `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/mod.rs` — remove the `pub mod prompt;` declaration.
+- `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/list.rs` and `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/mcp_integration.rs` — remove prompt-specific listing paths that feed the prompt command (keep skill/workflow listing).
+
+Keep: the CliContext prompt_library field is still needed for skill rendering at this stage — do NOT remove it here; that is handled in the later library-rename task. Only remove the user-facing `prompt` command surface.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/prompt/` directory no longer exists.
+- [ ] `sah --help` output contains no `prompt` subcommand.
+- [ ] `sah prompt` returns an unknown-subcommand error.
+- [ ] `cargo build -p swissarmyhammer-cli` succeeds.
+- [ ] Top-level CLI about/help text no longer markets "managing prompts".
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Update/remove `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/tests/integration/prompt_performance.rs` (delete — it tests the prompt command).
+- [ ] Add an integration assertion in the CLI test suite that running the binary with `prompt` exits non-zero and stderr mentions an unrecognized subcommand (e.g. in `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/tests/integration/`).
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-cli` is green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing "no prompt subcommand" assertion first, then delete code to make it pass.
\ No newline at end of file

--- original
+++ modified
@@ -44,4 +44,20 @@

 ## Notes / follow-ups
 - One pre-existing warning remains in dependency crate `swissarmyhammer-tools` (`unused variable: agent` in `mcp/tools/skill/use_op.rs`) — unrelated to this task, not touched.
-- `PromptSourceArg` (cli.rs) + `cli_conversions::PromptSource` are now only self-referential; left in place (public type, build.rs compiles cli.rs standalone) for the later library-rename task to address.
\ No newline at end of file
+- `PromptSourceArg` (cli.rs) + `cli_conversions::PromptSource` are now only self-referential; left in place (public type, build.rs compiles cli.rs standalone) for the later library-rename task to address.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-06-08 06:40)
+
+### Blockers
+- [ ] `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/tests/integration/mcp_integration.rs:21` — test_mcp_server_basic_functionality is a near-verbatim copy of the in-process MCP-server bootstrap scaffold (temp dir -> start server -> create_test_client -> list_tools -> assert non-empty -> cancel/shutdown) that already exists in two other tests. Three copies of this setup/teardown will drift out of sync — note one copy calls start_mcp_server while the other two call start_mcp_server_with_options, and the temp-dir rationale comment is re-pasted verbatim in each. Extract the shared bootstrap into a test_utils helper (e.g. `start_test_http_server() -> (server, client)` that allocates the temp dir, starts the in-process HTTP server, and returns a connected create_test_client). Each test then keeps only its distinct assertions/tool-call, parameterizing the tool op where they differ.
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/tests/integration/binary_aliases.rs:19` — Four parallel assert! blocks (lines 19-34) check output.contains(<cmd>) over the known set {serve, doctor, completion, validate}, differing only in the command-name constant and its message. These are N hand-maintained copies of one check — adding or renaming an expected command means editing another copy, and an omitted command silently goes untested. Express the expected commands as data and interpret with one loop: `for cmd in ["serve", "doctor", "completion", "validate"] { assert!(output.contains(cmd), "Help should mention {cmd} command"); }`.
+- [ ] `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/tests/integration/cli_integration.rs:21` — test_verbose_flag's only assertion `result.exit_code >= 0` is always true for the non-negative i32 exit_code, so the test cannot fail and verifies nothing about the --verbose flag. Assert the concrete outcome, e.g. `result.exit_code == 0` for a passing validate, and/or assert the verbose flag actually changes output.
+- [ ] `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/tests/integration/cli_integration.rs:34` — test_quiet_flag's only assertion `result.exit_code >= 0` is a tautology (exit_code is never negative), so the test passes unconditionally and does not exercise the --quiet flag. Assert a concrete exit code and/or that --quiet suppresses output (as the sibling test_root_validate_quiet already does at cli_integration.rs:88-93).
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/tests/integration/rmcp_stdio_working.rs:26` — The 'make temp dir → start_mcp_server_with_options(Http{port:None}, None, None, Some(temp)) → create_test_client(server.url())' isolation+startup block is duplicated inline rather than reused from a shared helper. test_utils is the canonical home for MCP test setup (it already owns create_test_client specifically to 'avoid code duplication and ensure consistent test client configuration'); the server-startup half should live there too, so the proxy/temp-dir/handshake conventions stay fixed in one place. Add a `start_test_server() -> (RunningService server, TempDir)` (or `start_test_server_and_client() -> (server, client, TempDir)`) helper in test_utils alongside create_test_client, and have these tests plus the four test_utils copies call it. The TempDir must be returned/held so it isn't dropped early.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/tests/rmcp_integration.rs:20` — test_mcp_server_with_rmcp_client is a near-verbatim copy of test_rmcp_client_lists_tools_and_prompts in tests/integration/rmcp_stdio_working.rs (~25-line shared scaffold: identical temp-dir + start_mcp_server_with_options HTTP setup, create_test_client, list_tools + non-empty assert, files-not-advertised assert, files tool call + non-empty content assert). The two differ only by the tool op/pattern (grep files/"test" vs glob files/"*.md") and one extra shell-not-advertised assert. A fix to the server-startup or tool-composition assertions in one will silently skip the other. Extract the shared scaffold into a helper in the test support module (e.g. `assert_unknown_host_lists_tools_and_calls_files(op: &str, pattern: &str)` returning the started server/client), and have each test call it with its op/pattern, then add the shell-gating assert only where it applies.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/test_utils.rs:11` — The module doc lists "Listing prompts" as a demonstrated usage pattern, but the `test_client_list_prompts` example test was deleted in this change. The doc now advertises an example that no longer exists. Remove the "- Listing prompts" bullet from the module-level doc comment.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/tests/integration/rmcp_stdio_working.rs:19` — The test is named `test_rmcp_client_lists_tools_and_prompts` and its doc comment claims it tests "prompt listing", but the prompts protocol surface was removed and the body no longer lists prompts — only tools. The name and doc now misdescribe what the test verifies, which misleads anyone diagnosing a failure. Rename to `test_rmcp_client_lists_tools` and drop the "prompts"/"prompt listing" phrases from the doc comment (lines 11, 16).
\ No newline at end of file
